name: Zero Cost LinkedIn Monitor (Google Alerts RSS)

nodes.name: Every 6 Hours
nodes.notes: Polls all configured RSS feeds every 6 hours. Adjust frequency as needed.

nodes.name: Config: RSS Feed URLs
nodes.notes: Edit this node to add/remove monitored people.

parameters.name: User-Agent
parameters.value: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/120.0.0.0 Safari/537.36

nodes.name: Fetch RSS via HTTP
nodes.notes: Fetches Google Alert RSS with a browser User-Agent. The built-in rssFeedRead node causes 404s on Google Alerts because it sends no User-Agent.

nodes.name: Parse RSS XML
nodes.notes: Parses the raw Atom/RSS XML into individual post items. Handles CDATA, Atom <link href>, and Google's redirect URL wrapping.

nodes.name: Deduplication (Skip Seen)
nodes.notes: Prevents double-commenting. State persists inside n8n — no database needed.

nodes.name: Filter: LinkedIn Posts Only
nodes.notes: Only passes through LinkedIn URLs (Google Alerts can surface other domains too).

nodes.name: Parse RSS Item
nodes.notes: Structures the RSS entry data for the AI Agent.

parameters.text:
=You are an Expert LinkedIn Networker and Thought Leader.
Your task is to write a short, engaging comment on a LinkedIn post.

Guidelines:
- Use simple words.
- Use a "Chris Do style from The Futur" — direct, insightful, grounded.
- **Be Concise:** Keep it under 50 words. Short and punchy.
- Do not make it too excited or use excessive exclamation marks.
- **Add Value:** Do not say "Great post" or "Thanks for sharing". Offer a specific insight, agreement, or polite counter-point.
- **Engage:** End with a short, open-ended question to encourage a reply.
- **Tone:** Professional but conversational. No hashtags. No emojis.

Context:
- Author: {{ $json.author }}
- Post URL: {{ $json.post_url }}
- Post Text (Google Alerts snippet): {{ $json.post_text }}

Write ONLY the comment text — no preamble, no labels.

nodes.name: OpenAI Chat Model
nodes.notes: Switch to gpt-4o-mini to reduce cost ~10x.

nodes.name: Extract Post URN
nodes.notes: Parses ugcPost/share/activity URN from the LinkedIn URL — no API call needed.
